pages.full_text:
policy seems to be that one woman does absolutely nothing, but point people where to go. The remaining woman had a queue of at least 7 people waiting to pay for things. She was incredibly slow. Afterwards she raced out of the shop to give Simon the tiny paper receipt. It was then that Simon realised he'd left his card in the machine. [marginal annotation: Cluny is quite like Sisteron or Aldeburgh - lots of tourists, which bring life, but also a town on its own]

Lunch was delicious. I had farm chicken. Simon had pancake - Galette with goats cheese & ham. We drove NW to __Montbard__ * * ** * a municipal one with a fantastic swimming pool. Again we had problems with money. The lockers charged 20p we lost the first 20p figuring out how to use it - you stuff clothes in a barrel which revolves vertically to close. You put in the barrel no., then your 4 digit made-up no. Then put money in, then close it. When you put the nos. in at the end, it springs open. Heaven help you if you forget the nos.
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread — no top of page visible. Marginal annotation in smaller script reads approximately 'Cluny is quite like Sisteron or Aldeburgh - lots of tourists, which bring life, but also a town on its own'. The word 'made-up' (as in made-up number) is written with a strikethrough or overwrite on 'made'. Montbard campsite star rating indicated by asterisks after the name. No handwritten page number visible on this (left) side.

pages.full_text:
The pool was amazing, indoor & out. 4 lanes for swimming in a big pool, a flume, a water shoot, battle of bubbles. Very enjoyable for everyone & free for campers. €17

For supper I did a salad - lettuce, tomato, cucumber, garlic, french dressing, cheese, pâté, lovely bread, wine, peach (v.good) apricot, plums & pastry. Simon's was meringue & butter cream, mine was a Strawberry Tart. And tea.

You can cycle from Montbard to Dijon along the canal. You can also cycle from campsite to canal, so would be a good base for cycling with a swim at the end. A bread van in the morning.

__Friday__ A long day 7-8 hours of driving, heading north. Eve drove one hour. We stopped at Le Clerke near Troyes & stocked up on wine & pâté. Sunshine. The road from Montbard → Tonnere not fast but pleasant & on to Chaouce. We reached the village next to Montreid & camped at __Le Pigeon Blanc__ * * in the village below-
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '21' visible top-right. 'Le Clerke' near Troyes — this may be a shop/supermarket name rather than a place name (Leclerc supermarket); transcribed as written. 'Tonnere' likely Tonnerre (town in Burgundy); transcribed as written. 'Chaouce' likely Chaource (village in Aube, known for cheese); transcribed as written. 'Montreid' uncertain — could read 'Montreid' or possibly 'Montréal' (village near Tonnerre); transcribed as written. 'Le Pigeon Blanc' appears to be a campsite name, underlined. Text continues onto next spread. No year explicitly stated on this page; context (€17, France route Montbard–Dijon–Troyes) consistent with volume B era (2003–2019); cannot narrow further from page content alone.
